description: The player awakens in the snow, recovering from a fall and realizing they are off the path, following fresh footprints leading into the trees.
The player lost 2 HP while moving carefully along the tracks.
A hidden tripwire disguised by snow caught the player's ankle, triggering a net made of icy vines that dropped from above and pinned the player.
Two figures emerge from the shadows carrying spears and identify the player as their captive.
The two figures tightened a net around the player, bound their wrists and ankles with coarse rope, and hoisted them to their feet to be led deeper into the forest.
The player and their captors arrive at a camp featuring crude lean-tos and a roaring bonfire, surrounded by figures in furs.
The player is brought before an older, commanding figure at a rough encampment, surrounded by wary captors.
The player attempts to speak in common and elven, but the villagers and elder respond with blank stares and dismissive grunts, indicating a language barrier.
The elder probed the bindings on the player's wrists and closely inspected their warlock attire and elven features.
The elder pulled dark fibrous material from a bowl and held it out to the player.
The elder examines the player's reaction while holding out dark fibrous material, and a guard clumsily loosens one of the player's bound wrists slightly.
The player attempts to grasp the dark fibrous material held by the elder but struggles due to stiff fingers and bindings, resulting in only brushing against the damp, sticky substance.
The elder signaled the captors to loosen the ropes, freeing the player's hands and feet, though they remain entangled in the net.
The elder signals approval by tapping his forehead and nodding, allowing the player to proceed with the ritual material despite their injury.
The net around the player no longer restricts their movement, freeing them to act.
The player applies dark fibrous material to their forehead, and a woman offers a bowl of clear, steaming broth.
A former captor untangles the icy vines of the net from around the player, freeing them completely.
The player drank the broth, restoring 2 HP.
